reasons: UAD full experiment shows F1=0.007 with only 2 true positives out of 541 detected pairs (precision=0.37%)
UAD F1_delta vs random baseline is NEGATIVE (-0.0001), meaning random selection performs marginally better
The F1=0.704 claim from Iteration 1 was on a different setup (35 same-cluster pairs, 19 TPs); the current 500-feature/50-cluster setup produces 3702 same-cluster pairs with only 2 TPs
DFDA's 99.5% improvement was confirmed as artifactual by all 6 result debate perspectives
DFDA parent-positive evaluation (E6) shows 21.2% on 1 pair only -- insufficient sample size
Cross-model validation (Gemma-2B, Pythia) was blocked in Iteration 1 and not retested in Iteration 2
All four H1-H4 hypotheses from Iteration 2 proposal are untestable with current evidence
No pretrained SAEs with <50% dead features were successfully loaded for H1/H3 testing
The clustering approach detects general co-occurrence, not absorption-specific structure
Methodologist's concern about unanchored metrics was correct -- and UAD fails even when anchored
next_actions: Diagnose UAD failure: test direct pair-search (O(n^2) phi thresholding) instead of clustering
Test if absorption signature (high P(child|parent), low marginal P(parent)) can be detected without clustering
If direct search also fails, pivot to Candidate B: co-occurrence as general SAE analysis tool with honest scope
Minimum viable fallback: methodological critique paper on collision rate != absorption and limitations of unsupervised detection
Consider whether the Chanin label set itself is too small (only ~19 absorption pairs) for meaningful evaluation
pivot_recommendation: Redesign UAD to use direct pair-search based on absorption signature (conditional probability + marginal frequency) rather than clustering. If that fails, pivot to co-occurrence characterization paper or critique paper.
